artists.name: Carolyn Elaine
artists.shortDescription: Chicago-based public artist creating permanent artworks that celebrate culture, community, and place.
artists.bio: Carolyn Elaine is a Chicago-based public artist whose work explores history, identity, and the stories that connect people to place. Working primarily in mosaic, she creates permanent installations that transform architecture into spaces of reflection, belonging, and cultural memory.

artists.name: Maya Rivers
artists.medium: Mixed media, painting, and digital collage
artists.shortDescription: Maya Rivers creates layered visual work exploring memory, movement, and urban landscapes.
artists.bio: Maya Rivers is a demo artist profile for The Galleria.Art. Her sample work blends painted surfaces, architectural fragments, and digital collage to suggest how city memory collects in windows, sidewalks, transit lines, and weathered facades. This profile is intentionally demo-friendly content for testing artist onboarding and gallery presentation workflows.

galleries.title: Carolyn Elaine Portfolio
galleries.description: A curated presentation of permanent public artworks, mosaic environments, and community-centered installations.

galleries.title: Urban Echoes
galleries.description: A sample gallery of mixed media works exploring rhythm, architecture, and city memory.

artwork.title: Whispers of Ancestral Wisdom
artwork.alt: Whispers of Ancestral Wisdom installation in St. Elizabeth, Jamaica
artwork.medium: Mosaic portraiture, relief sculpture, photography, and architecture
artwork.description: Created through community engagement and cultural research to honor the heritage of St. Elizabeth, Jamaica.

artwork.alt: Detail view of the central elder portrait
artwork.description: Detail view of the central elder portrait.

artwork.title: The Tears of Our Ancestors Irrigate Our Destiny
artwork.alt: The Tears of Our Ancestors Irrigate Our Destiny mosaic artwork
artwork.description: A community-engaged mosaic honoring civil rights history, resilience, civic participation, and hope.

artwork.title: We Carry the Light
artwork.alt: We Carry the Light permanent installation
artwork.description: A permanent installation celebrating the people, history, and cultural legacy of Bronzeville.

artwork.alt: Creating Our Own Narrative exterior mosaic
artwork.description: An exterior mosaic celebrating creativity, education, and the limitless potential of young people.

artwork.title: BJ's Mom
artwork.description: Created for a sensory learning environment, this mosaic celebrates storytelling, imagination, and discovery.

artwork.description: A stained-glass mosaic installation reflecting themes of faith, unity, and community.

artwork.title: Crosswalk Memory
artwork.alt: Demo placeholder artwork for Crosswalk Memory
artwork.description: Layered street markings and translucent forms suggest the repeated movement of people through familiar city intersections.

artwork.title: Gold Hour Window
artwork.alt: Demo placeholder artwork for Gold Hour Window
artwork.description: A study of late afternoon light, glass reflections, and the softened edges of buildings at the end of the day.

artwork.title: Blue Line Study
artwork.alt: Demo placeholder artwork for Blue Line Study
artwork.description: Rhythmic blue forms and architectural fragments reference transit routes, arrival, and the blur of daily motion.

artwork.title: After the Rain
artwork.alt: Demo placeholder artwork for After the Rain
artwork.description: Reflective surfaces and softened color fields evoke pavement, windows, and the city immediately after rainfall.

artwork.description: A draft demo record used to verify unpublished artwork stays out of public gallery pages.
